audit_items.proof_trace: lutil_b64_pton explicitly guards every write to target with if (target) checks (L213, L221, L232, L243, L295), handling NULL target safely; the only internally-computed pointer (pos = strchr at L207) is NULL-checked before use (L208). All server-side callers in passwd.c allocate and validate memory before invoking lutil_b64_ntop/lutil_b64_pton, and LDAP authentication passes the stored server-side password (not raw attacker-controlled input) to these functions.
